original_hypothesis.title: Arbitrary Execution via YieldRegistry Rescue Allows Admin to Drain User Funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `YieldRegistry.rescue` function allows the `POOL_ADMIN` to execute arbitrary calls (`target.functionCall(data)`) from any `YieldAccount`. A compromised or malicious admin can use this to transfer all staked assets (stETH, eETH, sDAI) out of every user's yield account.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While admins are trusted, providing them with a mechanism to arbitrarily execute code from user accounts violates the principle of least privilege and creates a massive single point of failure.
preconditions: Admin private key is compromised or admin acts maliciously

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Admin can call any function on any contract from the context of the user's YieldAccount
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Rescue functions should be restricted to specific tokens or have a timelock

analysis_questions: Is there a timelock on the POOL_ADMIN role?
Can the rescue function be restricted to only non-strategy tokens?
validation_requirements: Simulate an admin calling rescue to transfer stETH from a user's YieldAccount

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An attacker compromises the `POOL_ADMIN` private key. They iterate through all `YieldAccount` contracts and call `rescue` to execute `transfer` on the stETH contract, draining all user funds to their own wallet.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all user staked assets

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests for admin abuse of rescue function

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
